definition: Aggregate hyperscaler operating cash flow ($350-450B) exceeds capex ($250-300B) at a 1.5-1.7x coverage ratio, meaning AI infrastructure investment is self-funded rather than debt-financed. This structural difference from prior investment booms (e.g., telecom CLECs at >100% capex/OCF) prevents a Minsky sudden-stop from the hyperscaler layer.
evidence: Aggregate hyperscaler OCF ~$350-450B vs. capex ~$250-300B, coverage ratio 1.5-1.7x from public filings
Telecom CLECs were debt-funded at >100% capex/OCF — structurally different
All four pairwise debates confirmed this as the single most reliable claim
pair_0: 'the single most reliable claim across both analyses'
open_questions: At what capex/OCF ratio does self-funding become insufficient?
Does competitive pressure (prisoner's dilemma) override financial discipline before stress thresholds are reached?
